tldr: Caroline, 3 of your 5 live ads are near-identical 'Shop New Arrivals / ZII ROPA SS26' clones — that's a hook collision that typically lifts CPM 18-30% within 10-14 days. Two retail/pop-up ads are diluting the prospecting pool further with no product-led story carrying the SS26 launch.

benchmark.context: Slow-fashion peers running Japanese-fabric and small-batch positioning typically refresh creative every 22 days. Three duplicate SS26 New Arrivals ads pointing to the same /collections/new-women URL are accelerating fatigue well below the category median.

ads.headline: Shop New Arrivals
drivers: Exact duplicate of ad [1] — same headline, body, CTA, and landing URL competing in the same auction
Body copy 'ZII ROPA SS26' carries zero product or craft signal — no fabric, no silhouette, no reason to click
Third instance of the same /collections/new-women destination across the pool — Meta is pooling learnings against itself

ads.headline: Shop New Arrivals
drivers: Identical twin of ad [4] — duplicate creative bidding on overlapping audiences
No differentiation hook: 'SS26' alone won't stop the scroll for a cold prospector
Hook recycling — 3 of 5 ads in the pool open with 'Shop New Arrivals'

ads.headline: Shop New Arrivals
ads.body: ZII ROPA SS26  Japanese fabrics. Made in Mexico City.
drivers: Strongest of the three twins — 'Japanese fabrics. Made in Mexico City.' actually says something — but the headline is still recycled
Same /collections/new-women landing as two other ads — destination fatigue compounding
Craft story buried as a tagline instead of being the hook

ads.headline: Join us at our flagship store located at Tabasco 61, Roma Norte, CDMX
drivers: Body copy is a broken Liquid variable '{{product.brand}}' — this shipped untemplated and reads as a bug to anyone who notices
Headline doubles as the body — no narrative, no reason to learn more beyond an address
Retail-driver creative running in the same account as e-com prospecting with no audience separation

ads.body: Williamsburg, this week only ZII ROPA + ODISSEA pop-up 135 Wythe Ave April 30 – May 4 11–7PM
drivers: Headline field is empty — Meta will auto-pull from the landing page, which is a share.google redirect, so you're forfeiting the most valuable real estate on the placement
Pop-up ends May 4 — by the time fatigue hits, the offer is dead anyway
Landing goes to a Google share link instead of a branded ziiropa.com page, eroding trust signal

hero_concept.format: Static image (1:1) — first in a 3-part carousel test
hero_concept.hook: The Japanese mill behind your next favorite piece.
hero_concept.headline: Made to outlast the season
hero_concept.primary_text: Every SS26 piece begins with a fabric we couldn't find anywhere else — a limited run of Japanese linen, woven slowly, finished by hand in our Mexico City atelier. The Julia DeRose Ceramic is one of them: structured, breathable, designed to live in your closet for a decade. Limited cuts. No restocks.
hero_concept.visual_direction: Editorial still life of the Julia DeRose Ceramic piece on a raw linen surface beside a folded swatch of its source fabric, soft Mexico City morning light.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Four of your five live ads lead with destination ('Shop New Arrivals') or logistics (pop-up address) — none lead with the actual reason someone buys Zii Ropa: the fabric story and the craft. This concept replaces three duplicate SS26 ads with a product-led hook that earns the click instead of asking for it.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a quiet Mexico City atelier still-life with raw linen fabric draped on a warm-toned ceramic surface and a folded swatch of natural Japanese linen placed beside it, with soft diffused morning window light casting gentle shadows from the left. Muted earth-tone palette of bone, oat, and warm clay; minimalist slow-fashion aesthetic with intentional negative space and an artisanal, considered mood. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. Maintain the exact appearance, color, and details of the product from the reference image. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no text, no logos, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no buttons, no faces, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products.

next_step.urgency: Two ads hit fatigue inside 7 days — the duplicates need to come down this week.
next_step.headline: 15 min to map the SS26 refresh
next_step.body: I'll walk you through which ad to kill today, which one to scale, and a 3-concept shotlist built around the Japanese-fabric story. No pitch — just the plan.

method_note: Scoring is based on observable creative signals across the 5 live ads in Meta Ad Library; spend, CTR, and frequency data are not included.
